children.text: The West Side sits on the western edge of Stamford, and its biggest advantage is location. You’re close to downtown Stamford, close to the Greenwich border, and well-positioned for commuting—so it’s a neighborhood that tends to attract buyers who want to stay connected and keep logistics simple.

Housing on the West Side ranges from multi-family and townhouse-style inventory to pockets of single-family streets. That mix creates a neighborhood with broad appeal—whether someone is looking for a first home, an investment-minded purchase, or simply a place that’s close to work, train service, and daily necessities.

From a lifestyle standpoint, the West Side offers quick access to Stamford’s downtown dining and cultural venues, plus practical routes toward I-95 and the Stamford Transportation Center for Metro-North. It’s not a “sleepy” enclave—it’s a neighborhood that works well for people who prioritize access, flexibility, and getting the most out of Stamford’s location within Fairfield County.

highlights: Between downtown Stamford and the Greenwich line for strong regional positioning
Varied housing mix including multi-family, townhouse, and single-family options
Quick access to I-95 and major corridors for commuting
Easy drive to the Stamford Transportation Center for Metro-North rail access
Close to downtown restaurants, arts, and events without needing a downtown address
name: West Side
overview: Stamford’s West Side is a neighborhood that rewards convenience—positioned between downtown Stamford and the Greenwich line with quick access to major routes. The housing stock is varied, with a mix of multi-family properties and single-family streets depending on the block, and the overall feel is established and lived-in. It’s an area where daily life is efficient: shopping, commuting, and downtown amenities are all close. For buyers who want Stamford access with an easy NYC-oriented geography, the West Side is a practical choice.
